title: podman ask for repo when image or manifest is local but --platform is specified
body:
### Issue Description

When `--platform` option is supplied to `podman run`, it tries to pull remote image and only after `Ctrl+C` it reads local manifest or image. This makes it a pita to test locally built images before they are pushed to the target registry.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `buildah build --jobs=2 --platform=linux/amd64,linux/ppc64le --manifest multiarch-test .`
2. podman run --platform=linux/amd64 multiarch-test


### Describe the results you received

```
  ▸ registry.fedoraproject.org/multiarch-test:latest
    registry.access.redhat.com/multiarch-test:latest
    docker.io/library/multiarch-test:latest
    quay.io/multiarch-test:latest
```
Hit `Ctrl+C`.
```
Hello Reproducer!
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected is container to just run.
```
Hello Reproducer!
```

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Example Dockerfile
```
FROM alpine
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "-c", "echo 'Hello Reproducer!'"]
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @akostadinov!

This is the same issue as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17063 but with a different symptom.  As outlined in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17063, Podman will pull the image once --platform is specified.

It is not yet clear how to fix it though.  Previous attempts have caused regressions (e.g., https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1299).  A workaround would be to use `podman run --pull=never --platform ...`.

I am closing this issue here as duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17063.
